cases.label: bauxite
cases.name: Bauxite / alumina / aluminium
cases.choke: Alumina refining and energy-intensive smelting — China
cases.misread: "Australia controls bauxite" — but China refines ~59% of alumina; mine tonnage ≠ value-added control
cases.note: Atlas refined share (China 58%) matches IEA (59%); mine shares align (Australia and Guinea are neck-and-neck — the atlas lists Australia first, USGS 2024 Guinea first). The network layer correctly identifies China as the processing chokepoint despite its small export share.

cases.label: cobalt
cases.name: Cobalt
cases.choke: DRC mine concentration + Chinese refining/chemical processing
cases.misread: "DRC produces most cobalt" — true at the mine, but DRC refines almost none; usable cobalt is Chinese-processed
cases.note: Atlas has mine DR Congo 76%, refine China 76%, yet the top customs exporter is Finland (29%) — and the origin trace re-attributes it to DR Congo. The refiner illusion is captured exactly.

cases.label: graphite
cases.name: Natural graphite
cases.choke: Mining of usable grades + battery-grade processing — China
cases.misread: Assuming synthetic graphite fully substitutes — it competes only partly, and China leads that too
cases.note: Atlas: mine 78% / refine 95% / export 36%, all China-led, chokepoint China. Matches the authoritative picture closely.

cases.label: lithium
cases.name: Lithium
cases.ext_mine: Australia ~37%, Chile ~20%, China ~17%, Zimbabwe ~9%, Argentina ~8% (USGS 2024, Li content)
cases.misread: Australia is top miner, but spodumene is not battery-ready until converted (mostly in China); a mine-vs-export comparison ignores the product/conversion step
cases.note: Two findings, both now handled. (1) The traded HS code is lithium carbonate — Chile’s product (75% of trade) — so the mine-vs-export gap is a PRODUCT difference (spodumene vs carbonate), not a refiner illusion; the atlas now states this. (2) This audit caught a stale mine-share vintage (the atlas had Australia 52%); it is corrected here to USGS 2024 (Australia 37%, with Zimbabwe ~9% added).

cases.label: magnets
cases.name: Rare-earth permanent magnets (NdFeB)
cases.choke: Midstream separation + magnet fabrication — China; heavy REE (Dy, Tb) tighter still
cases.misread: Mine shares look diversified; separated and magnet-grade material is far more concentrated
cases.note: Atlas: mine China 69% / refine 90% / export 64%, all China-led; Myanmar (11%) is included and flagged as the worst-governance producer (g 0.84). Captured well.
